column1: # Determinism gate baseline (Q1 Stone 1, item 0). The proven-safe existing occurrences of the
# nondeterminism-vector patterns in the determinism-critical crates (core, physics, sim, world).
# The current tree is provably deterministic (the five canonical pins reproduce bit-exact across
# separate runs, which a state-feeding wall-clock or hash-order iteration would break); each site
# below is grandfathered with the reason it is determinism-safe. The gate (scripts/determinism_gate.py)
# fails on any count ABOVE its baseline (a new vector) and on any count BELOW it (update this file,
# with a reason, when a site is legitimately removed). Columns: pattern<TAB>path<TAB>count<TAB>reason.
# Rayon / par_iter is deliberately NOT a scanned pattern: the engine's parallel reductions are
# order-independent by design (the saturating-sum and min-fold, proven by the width-invariant pins),
# the sanctioned parallelism, not a hazard. This gate ratchets INTRODUCTION of a vector; it does not
# prove a grandfathered map stays lookup-only (that stays the reviewer's job at the site).

column4: profiling timer scope; the elapsed time is accumulated and reported to stderr, never read into canonical state
tick_timed profiling variant; per-phase nanoseconds are returned for a report, never state (the canonical tick is untimed)
StableId to EntityLocation lookup index (import, field, constructor); point-lookups by id only, never iterated into canonical state
StableId to Vec<EventId> lookup index (import, field); point-lookups by id only, never iterated into canonical state
calibration manifest String to ReservedValue lookups (import, field, constructor); require_* reads by key, never iterated into canonical state
